storyInfo: Rankin takes the miners who left the village with him and enter a mine in an attempt to circumvent a nearby outpost. There is no way out, but he finds many who have suffered and endured just like they have, who are willing to join the struggle.

attributes.content: Cold night, long night.
The bare earth is covered in frost. Upon it are haphazard footsteps, stretching into the distance where the sun fades beneath the horizon.
Scattered black dots are moving slow, at the place where the earth meets the night sky.
Lake Neva... is frozen.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: This year is bloody cold. It's not even winter yet... what are you laughing about?
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: I remember that in the winter of 31, Igor Velikiy defeated the army defending Grypherburg on Lake Neva, spelling the end of the Hippogryph Kingdom.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: And what about it? You want to lead us in an assault on Deity Grypherburg?
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: No. Not yet, at least.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Hmph... it's another story that I'm thinking of.
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: Around the year 150, a famous playwright wrote a play about Igor Velikiy before he entered Grypherburg.
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: His intention was to celebrate the friendship between Igor and the Hippogryph noble Mikhail, who were comrades in the revolution overthrowing the Hippogryph Kingdom.
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: But it was lambasted throughout Ursus because it mentioned a 'compromise' between Igor and certain Hippogryph nobles, whereby Igor gave up some power in exchange for their support.
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: The Ursus refused to accept it. They considered it a slander against Igor Velikiy.
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: The playwright paid the price for a story that he claimed to be true to history.
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: It was only many years after his death, when the original agreement between Igor and the Hippogryph nobles was discovered, that people learned he had been telling the truth.
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: A fine story.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Are you trying to tell me that the legend that I worship is just a figment of human imagination?
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: I'm trying to tell you that even Igor knew the importance of flexibility.
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: We should stop here, Rankin.
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: Grypherburg is in the way, and its defenses are on a completely different level from the Far North.
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: What if they find us? It won't be the Patrol Units waiting for us, but the Armies of Ursus.
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: Winter will find us in the wilderness if we continue to take detours and fail to reach the border in time.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: I would much rather deal with a patrol or two, than Ursine winter.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Do we really have to go to the Kazimierzian border, though? Find your legendary Reunion?
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: You know how far it is, never mind the enemies that we might encounter on the way. Look at our people, our food, even our clothes. Do we have enough to make it?
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: Let's say we actually make it to the border. What if it's not an Infected village that awaits us there, but another mine? What do we do then?
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: What other way is there? Where can we go?
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: There must be a way...
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: I know! We can go east! Nikolsk. It's an old city, almost abandoned now, and there are even fewer people in the surrounding villages.
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: We could go there, split up and hide in the villages... that's the only way for us to survive.
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: And live like slaves for the rest of our lives?
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: I gave you a choice, Nikto. I gave everyone a choice.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: I thought everyone chose to come with me to live like men.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: But you shouldn't wager everyone's lives on a castle in the sky—
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: I took a good look, Rankin.
attributes.name: Miner Scout

attributes.content: The outpost ahead is manned. The searchlights are on... it hasn't been abandoned.
attributes.name: Miner Scout

attributes.content: If we can't get through this way, do we have to...?
attributes.name: Miner Scout

attributes.content: You hear that, Rankin?
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: Searchlights... how many searchlights did you see?
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: I... I don't really remember. Two... maybe three?
attributes.name: Miner Scout

attributes.content: Think! How many exactly?
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Two! Yes, I remember now. There were only lights on the east and west sides. It's two lights!
attributes.name: Miner Scout

attributes.content: Two lights... so a garrison of a hundred or so. Maybe...
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Rankin!
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: Look behind you, Rankin... this is all the people we have...
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: This is not a revolutionary army. They just want to live.
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: Are you going to send them into the mines from which there is no return, like the mine owners did?
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: Rankin looks at the crowd behind him and sees their expressions under the lamplight.
Pain, fatigue, helplessness, fear...
Perhaps Nikto is right. Their clothes are too thin and they cannot take it. But he feels no cold, for some reason.
No...
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: I won't throw away any lives. Even if sacrifice may be inevitable, I won't allow anyone to die without meaning.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: We can't go back to a life of slavery...
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Follow me. There's another way.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: There's such a long mine tunnel underground?
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: What's going on, Rankin? How did you know where the mine tunnel was here?
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: Someone had an idea a few years back.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: There are more than a thousand mines in the Far North, each with hundreds of workers, yet they are all disconnected from each other. What if we changed that?
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: We could connect the tunnels—no one knows the underground better than we do. We could weave a web beneath the frozen soil and link everyone, sharing information and helping each other in the hour of need.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Have you been preparing all these years? Were you planning for this day?
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: I'm no prophet. I simply began with a very basic idea—we should unite and help each other.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: At the beginning, my influence extended only to a dozen mines in the area. I was powerless beyond that.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Later, I heard that other people were doing the same things in other places... it's a pleasant surprise to find that this tunnel looks relatively safe.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Where does it lead?
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: The Volgrad Mining District, southeast of Grypherburg, in the southernmost part of Ursus. If we can reach it through the tunnels, we'll be able to circumvent the capital's defenses.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: But you better pray there's no army on our tail, or it'll be an easy opportunity to wipe us out all at once.
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: No such thing as a path without risk.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Wait... I smell alcohol.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Impossible. How could there be—
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: Get down!
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: A flash of cold light lunges out from the darkness, breaking the only lit lamp.
What the—
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: Get down! Don't move, and don't fire back!
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Blyat! They found us!
attributes.name: Voice From the Tunnel

attributes.content: Call for help!
attributes.name: Voice From the Tunnel

attributes.content: Wait! Stop! We're friends!
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Rankin stands and shouts at the top of his lungs, his voice echoing in the deepest reaches of the cavern.
You...
attributes.name: Anxious Miner

attributes.content: You're not the Patrol Units?
attributes.name: Anxious Miner

attributes.content: That's what we were about to ask... I suppose the answer is no.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Thank goodness! I thought they found our position.
attributes.name: Anxious Miner

attributes.content: Where did you come from?
attributes.name: Anxious Miner

attributes.content: The Central Mining District.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Central? I didn't know there were any survivors there!
attributes.name: Anxious Miner

attributes.content: We heard the guards started a massacre. We thought there wasn't anybody left alive.
attributes.name: Anxious Miner

attributes.content: I can see why there would be such rumors...
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: But they're not true. We're still alive. We defeated the Patrol Units and escaped. We haven't given up on our struggle.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: That's the best news I've heard in a while!
attributes.name: Anxious Miner

attributes.content: One moment.
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: We're trying to get to the Volgrad Mining District, in the south, through these tunnels. Can we—
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: The south? It's no good. The tunnel leading to the south has collapsed. That's why we've been stuck here.
attributes.name: Anxious Miner

attributes.content: I see...
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: Again, Rankin! Again! Why is every path a dead end?
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: Wait... Rankin? THE Rankin?
attributes.name: Anxious Miner

attributes.content: My name's Rankin. I don't know anything about 'The' Rankin.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: It's really you! Rankin from Central! You're real—you're alive!
attributes.name: Excited Miner

attributes.content: I see my name has taken flight on its own...
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: We can talk about that later! Let me take you to the living area.
attributes.name: Excited Miner

attributes.content: You folks look like you're in real bad shape. We have some food and water, not a lot, but we can spare some.
attributes.name: Excited Miner

attributes.content: Living area? Are there a lot of you living here?
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Yes...
attributes.name: Excited Miner

attributes.content: A lot.
attributes.name: Excited Miner

attributes.content: They follow the lone light of the lamp and walk into the tunnel. Gradually, they begin to see the light at the end, and hear the faint sound of talking.
They go around a corner to see a large number of people—there must be hundreds of them—living in a tiny space.
Some are wearing miner's outfits, others simple cotton clothes. There are elderly, and there are children... with simple living tools around them.
This is where they live.
You...
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: What's going on? Didn't you guys go on patrol? Why did you bring so many people back? Who are they?
attributes.name: Worried Miner

attributes.content: Friends! Let me introduce this man here—Rankin!
attributes.name: Excited Miner

attributes.content: Rankin! He's Rankin!
attributes.name: Excited Miner

attributes.content: I've heard the name before! You were the one who had the idea of linking all the mining districts!
attributes.name: Curious Miner

attributes.content: You're not as tall as the stories say, but I guess you're tall enough...
attributes.name: Hunched Old Man

attributes.content: Did you fight the Kazimierzians? Do the Firewatch really exist?
attributes.name: Young Child

attributes.content: They rush forward to see the figure of legend. There is reverence, there is joy, and there is a little bit of disappointment in seeing the real man behind the myth, but all culminate in an excited call for his name.
Rankin, Rankin.
Alright, that's enough. Our friends have run into some trouble and they need rest.
attributes.name: Excited Miner

attributes.content: Get some bread from the storage room, Zoya. The big loaves.
attributes.name: Excited Miner

attributes.content: ...Why are you living here?
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: That's a long story...
attributes.name: Excited Miner

attributes.content: The Neva Dam burst three years ago, flooding the nearby mines. The overseer refused to let us come out from underground, fearing that we might flee to a nearby city.
attributes.name: Excited Miner

attributes.content: The mine eventually collapsed, splitting it in half. Half of us were drowned. We were the lucky half.
attributes.name: Excited Miner

attributes.content: We thought all we could do was wait for death, until we found a passage below that led to another mine.
attributes.name: Excited Miner

attributes.content: We got some food from there, but we didn't dare go above ground, so we stayed underground...
attributes.name: Excited Miner

attributes.content: And you've been living here? How?
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: We helped deliver cargo for other mines. Some kind-hearted people shared food with us. Sometimes we stole from the Patrol Units... just like musbeasts, eh.
attributes.name: Excited Miner

attributes.content: We continued to expand the passages, to the Ural in the east and Kras in the west, linking up a lot of mines and districts.
attributes.name: Excited Miner

attributes.content: We've taken in refugees who fled the other mines. We've got maybe a thousand people who are somewhat fit to move.
attributes.name: Excited Miner

attributes.content: There are children here...
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Yes... she's two years-old, and she's never seen the sun...
attributes.name: Worried Mother

attributes.content: Blyat!
attributes.name: Calm Miner

attributes.content: No matter what, we're still alive! All thanks to you, Rankin!
attributes.name: Excited Miner

attributes.content: We'd have been long dead if you didn't come up with your idea!
attributes.name: Excited Miner

attributes.content: No... I did nothing. Don't thank me. You found your path of life on your own.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: But it shouldn't have been like this. This shouldn't have been your life.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: But... what could we do, Rankin?
attributes.name: Excited Miner

attributes.content: Of course we don't want to live in this hole forever, but where else can we go?
attributes.name: Excited Miner

attributes.content: ...Come with me.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Where to?
attributes.name: Excited Miner

attributes.content: South.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: To the Kazimierzian border. We'll find the village that belongs to the Infected, land that we can live on.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: We'll go together.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Calm down, Rankin.
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: Did you forget about the Lake Neva Outpost? Did you think you could sneak past them with so many people?
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: Then we'll take it by force.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: You're out of your mind—
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: I know what I'm talking about, Nikto!
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: I'm confident.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: The Lake Neva Outpost will have at most a hundred men, and no heavy weapons.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Three hundred. Give me three hundred pairs of hands that can hold a crossbow and a hoe, and we'll take the Lake Neva Outpost before they have a chance to send a distress signal.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: By the time the regular supply delivery arrives weeks later, we'll be long gone, on the vast Barton Plains!
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: We'll only be one step away from the goal. Nothing can stop us.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: No... you've gone mad.
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: I don't understand... why do you always force yourself into the riskiest option? Why do you—
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: Rankin ignores his objections and extends his hand to the crowd.
I see your vision, Rankin, and I'm willing to go with you to take the outpost.
attributes.name: Excited Miner

attributes.content: But I can't make the others follow you. They'll have to decide their own fates.
attributes.name: Excited Miner

attributes.content: The miner shakes Rankin's hand and goes to stand behind him.
All who are willing to go with Rankin to take the outpost, come forward.
attributes.name: Excited Miner

attributes.content: Feet shuffle in the crowd, and several youths take their place behind Rankin.
Others remain hesitant, or find their own family hanging onto their shirts.
Whatever the others say, I'm with you, Rankin.
attributes.name: Calm Miner

attributes.content: I don't know what that place for the Infected on the Kazimierzian border is like, but it can't be worse than here.
attributes.name: Calm Miner

attributes.content: We trust you because you're Rankin. You're the hero that united us. No matter what end awaits us—
attributes.name: Calm Miner

attributes.content: Rankin halts those words by raising a hand. He looks down, takes a deep breath, then looks up again at the crowd, his eyes resolute.
Have you ever seen spring in Ursus?
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Silence.
I have, when I was a soldier with the border guard.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: When the snow melts, green grass grows from the soil again, and the migratory fowlbeasts come back.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Sometimes, the sun is out all day, so bright it almost drives you mad, and you can only sleep by pulling your blanket over your head.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: The sun, the grass, the fowlbeasts, the wind... none of those exist in the mines.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: But it's not because we don't deserve it!
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: It's not too much to ask. These are things that you and I, your children, all of us, deserve as human beings!
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Rankin's words echo in the dark mine, and in the hearts of all who are listening.
It will be dangerous. I won't lie to you, nor will I force anyone to join us.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: But sacrifices are a part of any struggle. There will always be those who fall by the wayside, or leave of their own accord.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Whether they fell or left on their own, whether they were brave or timid, remember those who are around you, and remember that you once stood together and shared the same pain.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: We will always be comrades.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Some may fall tonight, and some may live. Those who live will pave the path to tomorrow for the rest.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: We shall raise a torch upon the outpost to remember the fallen, that survivors may remember, and the oppressor will see.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Before we leave, I want to tell you how I feel.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: I hope you will join me and contribute your strength.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Only by taking this step can we go back to the surface, bathe in the sunlight, and become human again!
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Rankin's chest rises and falls. He feels hot blood flow through his veins, and his strength return to him, and he raises his arms as he speaks.
More and more people come over to him, as they did when he first made his rallying cry in the mine shafts of the Central Mining District.
Brothers, sisters, remember the inhuman lives that we once led.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: We were herded into the mines like beckbeasts, using our lives to dig for Originium to warm the bed of others. This is not the law; it is murder.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Yes, murder. They tried to shut our mouths, keep us captive until we die, stop us from thinking and make us numb, until we are no different from animals.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: But we did not forget how to struggle. Deep underground, we connected the mines, and broke through our prison of death.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: But that's not enough. We can't just keep running away. We must face our foe head-on.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Someone might ask, 'What do we do, Rankin?'
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: It's difficult, but it is also easy. The first thing to do is take the first step.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Tonight's battle will be the first step. More people will learn who we are, and all those who are oppressed like we are will know that they're not alone!
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Do not fear. There's no need to fear. Those who are high above are not strong. We are strong!
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: We have nothing to lose except our lives... if they won't give us our right, then we'll fight for it with our lives!
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Ask me. Ask me, 'What do we do?' 'What will become of us?'
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: History has shown us time and again that it doesn't forgive oppressors. If punishment has not yet come, it's only because it still needs time, and anger that will set everything on fire.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Igor Velikiy ripped the wings off the high-and-mighty Hippogryphs. If his descendants are doing the same things the Hippogryphs once did, then we will drag them down from their thrones!
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Our weapons will once again force Deity Grypherburg's golden gates open. Our pickaxes will dig a hole in this land, and tear its ugly, greedy face to pieces!
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: All of Ursus will see us, fear us, recognize us.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Then, we will have a bright future.
attributes.name: Rankin
